security_requirements.description: cpp-LIN does not implement authentication (no network connectivity); integrating system must authenticate LDF and configuration files via secure boot hash (DSR-05).
No default passwords or credentials exist in cpp-LIN. No authentication secrets stored in source code.
security_requirements.evidence: Source code review — no credential strings

security_requirements.description: cpp-LIN enforces frame ID boundaries (0x00–0x3F) at all API entry points. No frame is processed beyond declared protocol bounds.
Wireless access is not applicable; LIN is a wired single-master bus. Physical access control is integrator responsibility (ASM-09).
Communication integrity for safety-critical payloads is enforced by CRC-16/CCITT-FALSE E2E protection header (lin::safety::Protector/Receiver).
Replay attacks are detected by the monotonic sequence counter in the E2E safety header. Receiver rejects any non-sequential counter.
Software and information integrity for LDF files is an integrator responsibility (secure boot hash verification). cpp-LIN validates LDF parse errors but cannot verify file provenance.
Error detection on all transmitted frames: PID parity bits (P0/P1), enhanced checksum, and optional E2E CRC protection.
LIN bus payloads are not encrypted (SL-2 does not require encryption for low-speed automotive buses). Confidentiality is an integrator responsibility for high-sensitivity data.
security_requirements.notes: LIN bus confidentiality requires hardware encryption at transceiver level; outside cpp-LIN scope

security_requirements.description: No network communication in cpp-LIN. Data flows only over the LIN bus (physical layer) and through in-process API calls.
security_requirements.evidence: Architecture: no sockets, no HTTP, no MQTT in library code

security_requirements.description: Audit logging is not built into cpp-LIN. Integrating system must implement event logging (e.g., ErrNoResponse, E2EError) for incident detection.
security_requirements.notes: OnError callback (REQ-MASTER-007) provides the hook for integrator logging

security_requirements.description: Vulnerability reporting and patching follows the process defined in INCIDENT-RESPONSE.md (SLA: Critical 7d patch, High 30d patch).
cpp-LIN uses bounded channels (Chan<T> with configurable depth) with backpressure policies (DropNewest, DropOldest, Block) to prevent resource exhaustion under high load.
Watchdog timer to detect schedule runner starvation is an integrating system responsibility (DSR-06). cpp-LIN provides the stop flag mechanism for graceful shutdown.
Security vulnerability management process defined in INCIDENT-RESPONSE.md and SECURITY.md, including private reporting, SLA, ASIL impact assessment, and coordinated disclosure.
TARA performed for cpp-LIN SEooC scope. See TARA.md and tara.json for full threat catalogue, CVSS scores, controls, and residual risk acceptance.
Security requirements derived from TARA are traced to implementation via fusa:req annotations in source code and .fusa-reqs.json.
Static analysis (clang-tidy) runs on every PR, checking for bugprone-*, clang-analyzer-*, memory safety patterns. Errors are CI gates.
Dynamic analysis (ASan + UBSan + ThreadSanitizer) runs on every PR as CI gates. Halt-on-error is enabled.
Security-relevant test cases are tagged with fusa:test annotations and referenced in .fusa-reqs.json. Coverage gate >=70% enforced in CI.

open_items.description: Fuzz testing for validate_frame() and ldf::parse() with AFL++ not yet implemented
SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) generation not yet automated in CI
MISRA C++ compliance check not yet integrated
